sections.Goal: Build a two-sided marketplace connecting [SELLERS] with [BUYERS] in the [NICHE] vertical. The system handles listing management, matching, payments via Stripe Connect, and trust/safety autonomously.
sections.Why: Marketplaces benefit from network effects — value increases as both sides grow. Once critical mass is achieved, the platform becomes self-sustaining. Commission-based revenue means zero inventory risk and income scales with transaction volume.
sections.What: A marketplace platform with: seller onboarding and listing management, buyer search and discovery with AI-powered matching, Stripe Connect for split payments and escrow, review and reputation system, dispute resolution workflow, and admin dashboard for monitoring.
sections.Context: Tech stack: Next.js 15 (App Router), Stripe Connect for split payments, PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM, Vercel for hosting. Follow Golden Rules — Governance First (human approval for dispute resolutions over threshold), Human-in-the-Loop (flag suspicious listings for review), and Fractal Organization (marketplace operates independently with own monitoring).
sections.Blueprint: Phase 1: Core marketplace — seller listings, buyer search, and basic profiles. Phase 2: Stripe Connect integration — split payments, seller payouts, escrow for services. Phase 3: AI matching — recommend listings to buyers based on preferences and history. Phase 4: Trust and safety — review system, fraud detection, automated dispute resolution. Phase 5: Growth tools — featured listings, analytics for sellers, promotional campaigns.
sections.Validation: Level 1: TypeScript compiles, ESLint passes. Level 2: Unit tests for matching algorithm, payment splits, and listing logic. Level 3: Integration test — seller lists, buyer purchases, payment splits correctly, seller receives payout. Level 4: Stripe Connect onboarding test — verify seller account creation and verification flow. Level 5: Dispute resolution test — verify escalation and resolution workflow.

validationGates.name: Syntax & Types
validationGates.description: Ensure code compiles and passes linting

validationGates.name: Unit Tests
validationGates.description: Run all unit tests with coverage reporting

validationGates.name: Marketplace Flow Test
validationGates.description: End-to-end test of listing, purchase, and payout flow

validationGates.name: Stripe Connect Test
validationGates.description: Verify seller onboarding, payment splits, and payout scheduling

commonPitfalls: Solving the chicken-and-egg problem — seed one side of the marketplace before launching the other
Not handling Stripe Connect onboarding failures — sellers abandon if KYC flow is confusing
Missing escrow for services — buyers need protection when paying for work not yet delivered
Ignoring fraud detection — marketplaces attract bad actors; implement automated flagging early
Setting commission rates too high initially — keep take rates low to attract early adopters
Not building trust signals — reviews, verification badges, and response times are critical for conversion
